title: Kissing Usury
content:
Biancha, let
Me pay the debt
I owe thee for a kiss
Thou lend'st to me;
And I to thee
Will render ten for this.

If thou wilt say,
Ten will not pay
For that so rich a one;
I'll clear the sum,
If it will come
Unto a million.

He must of right,
To th' utmost mite,
Make payment for his pleasure,
(By this I guess)
Of happiness
Who has a little measure.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Robert Herrick delivers a powerful performance in "Kissing Usury"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
